tools.name: future_value
tools.description: Calculate future value of an investment with compound interest. Use this for investment growth projections, savings account growth, and retirement planning. For comparing multiple rates and periods, use future_value_table instead.

rate.description: Annual interest rate as decimal (e.g., 0.05 for 5%)

years.description: Number of years for the investment

tools.name: present_value
tools.description: Calculate present value (current worth) of a future amount. Use this for loan valuation, bond pricing, and investment analysis. For comparing multiple scenarios, use future_value_table with different periods and rates instead.

rate.description: Annual discount rate as decimal (e.g., 0.05 for 5%)

years.description: Number of years until the amount is received

tools.name: discount
tools.description: Calculate final price after applying a percentage discount. Use this for retail pricing, sale calculations, and cost savings analysis. For comparing multiple discount percentages, use discount_table instead.

discount.description: Discount percentage (e.g., 20 for 20% or 0.20 as decimal, depending on input format)

tools.name: markup
tools.description: Calculate selling price from cost and markup percentage. Use this for product pricing, profit margin calculation, and business pricing strategy. For comparing multiple markup scenarios, use discount_table which also shows profit margins.

markup.description: Markup percentage (e.g., 30 for 30% or 0.30 as decimal, depending on input format)

tools.name: future_value_table
tools.description: Generate a comparison table of future values across multiple interest rates and time periods. Use this for investment scenario comparison, rate shopping, and long-term planning. For single calculations, use future_value instead.

periods.description: Time periods in years to compare (e.g., [1, 5, 10, 20])

tools.name: discount_table
tools.description: Compare multiple discount percentages for the same price. Use this for bulk pricing strategies, promotional planning, and price comparison. For single discount calculations, use discount instead.

tools.name: launch_ui
tools.description: Launch the interactive web UI for the financial calculator. Use this for visual, tab-based calculations with real-time results and beautiful gradient UI. For command-line calculations, use the individual calculation tools instead.

port.description: Port number for the web server (default: 5050)

messages.content: Hey, I was looking at some Noah Cyrus merch online and I wanted to figure out how much it'd cost with a discount. Like if something is $50 and there's a 20% off sale, what would I actually pay? Can you help me figure that out?
Sure! Let me calculate that for you.

With a 20% off discount on a $50 item, you'd pay **$40** — the discount saves you $10! 

Let me know if you're looking at other Noah Cyrus merch and want to calculate prices for different items or discount rates.

Oh that's super helpful, thanks! Okay so like, I've been thinking about saving up for concert tickets and I was wondering if you could help me figure out how my savings could grow over time? Like if I start with some money and keep adding to it, how much would I have later? I'm trying to save for tickets to see my favorite artist.

I'd be happy to help you figure out how your savings could grow! A few questions to make sure I calculate this right:

1. **How much are you starting with?** (Your initial savings amount)
2. **How long are you planning to save?** (In years)
3. **What interest rate do you expect?** (For savings accounts, it varies — right now maybe around 3-5% APY?)

Also, will you be adding money regularly (like monthly contributions), or just starting with a lump sum and letting it grow?

Let me know and I can run the numbers for you!

Oh wait, before we get to the savings thing, can we go back to the merch discounts? I was thinking like, what if there are different sales happening at the same time? Like if something is $50 and there's 20% off, but then there's also maybe a 15% discount or something — how do I figure out which deal is better? Can you compare a few different discount options for me?
Totally! Let me compare a few different discount options for you so you can see which deal gives you the best price.

Here's a comparison for your $50 merch:

| Discount | You Pay | You Save |
|----------|---------|----------|
| 10% off  | $45.00  | $5.00    |
| 15% off  | $42.50  | $7.50    |
| 20% off  | $40.00  | $10.00   |
| 25% off  | $37.50  | $12.50   |
| 30% off  | $35.00  | $15.00   |

So the 30% off deal is the best — you'd only pay $35 and save $15 total! The 20% deal we started with saves you $10, which is solid, but if you can find a bigger discount, that's obviously better.

Want me to check any other discount percentages?

Ohhh that table is so helpful, love seeing all the options at once! Btw, is there a web version of this calculator I can try out myself? I'd love to play around with it and see what other features you've got.
Yes, there is! Let me launch the web interface for you.

I've launched the web calculator for you! You can access it at:

**http://127.0.0.1:5050**

It has a nice visual interface where you can:
- Calculate discounts (like we just did)
- Do future value calculations for your savings
- Compare different rates and periods side-by-side

Feel free to play around with it and explore the different tools. Let me know if you have questions about the savings stuff or anything else!

Oh sick, thanks for launching that! I'm gonna go check it out now and play around with the different calculators. I'll let you know if I have any questions when I start figuring out the savings stuff! 🎵

Awesome, have fun exploring it! 🎉

Whenever you're ready to figure out your concert ticket savings plan, just come back and let me know the details (how much you're starting with, how long you're saving, that kind of thing) and we'll work through it together. 

Enjoy checking out the calculator! 🎵
